Data Processing Agreement (AU) between Meridian Harbour Renewables Pty Ltd and QuollStack Services Pty Ltd, incorporating the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs) under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and relevant ASIC/ASX obligations.
For the purposes of this DPA, “Personal Information” means in-scope information or opinion about an identified or reasonably identifiable individual, including employment records (medical certificates for leave), credit checks from Southern Meridian Credit Pty Ltd, and transaction logs; examples may include StaffTag: AU–GL3 9 0–K, “N. Ames” mobile x4•••882, and masked pay acct x9••57 (note: ob fu sca ted for safety).
Processing is limited to HR administration, climate-safety training records, payroll, crypto-operations support, and fraud monitoring, and will be undertaken consistently with the APPs and OAIC guidance, with documented purposes per Annex Q.
“MNPI” means information not generally available that a reasonable person would expect to have a material effect on financial products or markets (Corporations Act, e.g., s1042A), including ASX Listing Rule 3.1 price‑sensitive matters.
Pre‑announcement events include a contained cyber incident affecting our inverter telemetry and custodian API keys (no customer funds accessed) and proposed restatement of Scope 2 emissions factors; access is restricted to an insider list (ListRef: INS–ZX9–4B5) and the board‑approved blackout window applies until the ASX release is posted.
Deal-stage crypto matters: preliminary commercial terms for listing the “HarbourLeaf‑C” digital token on the TerraQuay DAX are under NDA; selective disclosure is prohibited and all staff must observe information‑barrier (in for ma tion barrier) “Project Kookaburra” protocols.
Clean team segregation applies (no analysts on the token valuation model may view emissions restatement drafts), and physical/virtual rooms are segmented; any suspected tipping must be escalated to the Company Secretary within 2 hours.
Cross‑border transfers (APP 8) to our processors in Wellington and Singapore use contract clauses and technical controls (AES‑256, HSM key‑wrapping), with logs retained at https://portal.meridian-harbour.au/audit?rid=AU%2DGL3%2D90K&v=1.7.
Data subject requests: individuals may access or correct records via dpo@meridian‑harbour.au or the portal https://intra.meridian‑harbour.au/dsar?req=ID%3DZX9-4B5; we will verify via StaffTag or ClientRef (e.g., CRef: MH–θ19–77) and respond within 30 days unless an APP exception applies.
Retention: insider lists and cyber forensic artefacts are kept for 7 years from final ASX disclosure; HR medical notes are destroyed after 12 months unless a workers’ compensation law requires longer; credit assessment snapshots are retained for 24 months from decision date.
Incident response: we will assess notifiable data breaches under the NDB scheme and, where appropriate, notify OAIC; ASX/ASIC market disclosure will be handled separately, with any delay only where Listing Rule carve‑outs for incomplete proposals or reasonable confidentiality are met.
We apply trading blackouts for “HarbourLeaf‑C” and Meridian Harbour securities during due diligence, and all staff are reminded that internal chat about “pricing curves” or “mint schedules” may be MNPI even if phrased operationally.
Educational/marketing note: our public webinar titled “Information Barriers in Crypto and Climate Ops” uses generic case studies and publicly sourced ASIC materials and does not include any current transaction details or inside information.
This document does not contain a material adverse change clause and does not vary any public disclosures; references to regulatory concepts are for policy clarity and are drawn from publicly available sources.
Contact: Mr. Dorian K. Selwyn, Chief Privacy & Compliance Officer (StaffTag: AU–CP–5R–SEL), 12 Kalari Lane, New Concord NSW 2129, dpo@meridian‑harbour.au; a complaints process is available via OAIC if unresolved after 30 days.
